text.title: EVM Process and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Four-step EVM implementation process, common measurement mistakes, and agent-specific pitfalls for automated EVM pipelines.

list.item: Step 1 — Establish Baseline: create a time-phased PV curve (planned spend per period); freeze as BAC with sponsor sign-off
Step 2 — Measure Progress: per reporting period, collect objective % complete for each WBS element; calculate EV = BAC x %complete; record actual AC
Step 3 — Calculate Metrics: SV, CV, SPI, CPI, EAC, ETC, VAC, TCPI per WBS element and rolled up to project
Step 4 — Forecast and Act: trend analysis over 3+ periods; variance analyst flags sustained CPI/SPI below threshold; PM decides corrective action; re-baseline only with CCB approval

rule.statement: Track EVM at WBS level 3-4; not at the leaf task level and not at the project summary level only.
rule.rationale: Leaf-level tracking generates noise from individual task estimation error. Summary-level tracking hides which work packages are in trouble until the overall CPI collapses.

rule.statement: Treat re-baselining as a P1 event; log it, require CCB approval, never automate.
rule.rationale: Silent re-baselining is the most common way teams game EVM. A team with CPI 0.70 can re-baseline to show CPI 1.00 overnight. Require an explicit decision record for every re-baseline.

antipattern.description: Re-baselining to "fix" red metrics without CCB approval.
antipattern.reason: Hides true variance. A project that has re-baselined three times has three sets of lies as its history. Future forecasts become meaningless.

antipattern.description: Ignoring early CPI warnings because "it will recover".
antipattern.reason: CPI rarely improves on its own — the "CPI cliff" is empirically documented. A CPI of 0.80 at 20% complete predicts final CPI of 0.70-0.85 in most projects.

antipattern.description: Reporting SPI/CPI numbers without a 3-period trend line.
antipattern.reason: A single-period metric value tells you nothing about direction. Stakeholders need trajectory, not a snapshot. Always show last 3 periods together.

rule.statement: Enforce single base currency; agents must not silently mix USD/EUR/GBP in AC.
rule.rationale: Currency mixing produces nonsense CPI values. Multi-currency programs need FX-hedged EAC with explicit conversion date and rate.

list.item: % complete from PR counts or story point burn is a proxy — calibrate against milestone-based EV gates
AI tooling cost (token spend) is a real AC component; meter it and include in AC if material
Effort vs cost confusion: hours × role rate; agents must pull rate-per-role, not a flat blended rate
LLM narratives mask negative variance with euphemism; require numeric thresholds before any positive wording is permitted in status reports
EAC = BAC/CPI is pessimistic for early-stage projects; allow EAC = AC + (BAC - EV)/(CPI × SPI) for schedule-sensitive items
